About

Building systems that scale and teams that thrive

For over fifteen years, I've been at the intersection of ambitious engineering challenges and practical, elegant solutions. From Idaho Falls to Hillsboro to Portland, my career has been a through-line of tackling the hard infrastructure problems that others shy away from.

At Intel and McAfee, I've shipped CI/CD pipelines, cloud platforms, and distributed databases relied upon by enterprise customers worldwide. I've directed teams, filed patents, and mentored engineers across every stage of their careers — all while staying hands-on with the code that matters most.

I believe great software is built by great people working with clarity of purpose. I bring both the technical depth and the collaborative instincts to make that happen.

Experience

Mar 2022 — Dec 2025
Intel Corporation
Hillsboro, OR
Cloud Software Development Engineer

Led the design and delivery of Intel's Deepfake Detector platform — directing a team of four engineers to build a Python API + React UI system capable of processing 30 simultaneous video streams per node, ultimately delivered to 10 external customers. Architected C++ cloud infrastructure for an immersive 3D video conferencing system, streaming from 15 cameras to GPU worker nodes via gRPC with sub-100ms end-to-end latency. Engineered CI/CD pipelines on AWS EKS using GitHub Actions and Terraform, slashing deployment times to minutes and enabling safer, faster releases. Also led the migration of Intel Foundry Services C# applications from Windows VMs to Linux/Kubernetes, delivering hundreds of automated reports to foundry customers every day.

Apr 2017 — Mar 2022
McAfee Enterprise
Hillsboro, OR
Senior Software Engineer

Drove the SIEM database platform to achieve over one million events per second — making it the fastest SIEM in the market — by deploying on AWS using Docker on EC2 with Kafka streaming containers. Built a Python health monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Elasticsearch, Kibana) providing full backend observability for hundreds of enterprise customers. Established a TeamCity CI pipeline with 85%+ code coverage and tens of thousands of daily test runs, enabling confident daily releases. Provided technical leadership across roadmap planning, architecture decisions, and the mentoring of incoming engineers.

Jan 2015 — Apr 2017
Intel Security Group
Idaho Falls, ID
Software Engineer

Designed and developed the core Java and C++ components of a new proprietary SIEM database engine — a distributed system performing consistency checks in seconds and synchronizing millions of records in minutes. Developed patented database technologies covering data consistency, replication, distributed retrieval, and flexible data structures. Resolved high-severity production incidents using Linux tooling including gdb, tcpdump, strace, and log analysis.

Jun 2011 — Dec 2014
Nitro Security
Idaho Falls, ID (McAfee acquisition)
Software Engineer II

Developed custom C++ interfaces for the Nitro SIEM database engine, and built a database health analysis and repair tool for support engineers that reduced diagnostic and repair tasks from hours to minutes.

Apr 2009 — Jun 2011
AMX International
Rexburg, ID
Software Developer II

Lead developer on Oracle CRM SaaS integrations, building Java web services to synchronize customer data reliably with cloud services.

Beginnings

Beyond the Code

Rooted in family, driven by purpose

The same values that make me a reliable, collaborative engineer — commitment, patience, and care for the people around me — are ones I learned and practice at home. I'm a husband and father of three boys, and life in the Pacific Northwest suits us well. The mountains, the coast, and the community of Portland have shaped who I am outside of work.

The technical achievements on my resume don't exist in isolation. Behind every deployment pipeline and distributed system is a person who shows up every day motivated by more than just the work itself.
